seo.title: Vercel AI SDK Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for Vercel AI SDK agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using Vercel AI SDK face heightened compliance risks due to AI-generated product recommendations, pricing displays, and customer interactions that must adhere to strict FTC, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS, and ADA regulations. Without guardrails, streaming responses can hallucinate unsubstantiated claims or inaccurate pricing, leading to FTC enforcement, refund disputes, and revenue loss. Vex provides middleware to validate AI outputs in real-time, ensuring compliance across providers and edge functions.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims must be substantiated with evidence; AI cannot generate misleading promotions or unverified testimonials.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex middleware scans streaming responses for unsubstantiated claims, blocking or correcting hallucinations before UI render.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data handling in personalized recommendations requires consent and accurate data usage disclosure.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Validates PII redaction and consent checks in structured tool calls, ensuring compliant personalization.

complianceRequirements.description: Payment-related AI responses must not expose card details or suggest insecure transactions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Blocks sensitive payment data in streams and validates tool call results before display.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated UI text must be accessible, with proper alt text and readable language for screen readers.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Enforces WCAG-compliant language patterns and flags inaccessible phrasing in real-time.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated Product Claims
industryRisks.description: AI fabricates benefits like 'clinically proven' without evidence, violating FTC rules.
industryRisks.example: Chatbot claims 'Our moisturizer reduces wrinkles by 90%' based on hallucinated study.

industryRisks.name: Inaccurate Pricing Display
industryRisks.description: Streaming UI shows wrong prices due to provider inconsistency, causing refund disputes.
industryRisks.example: Product page streams $49.99 as $29.99 mid-response, leading to chargeback claims.

industryRisks.name: Unvalidated Refund Policy
industryRisks.description: Tool calls return incorrect return policy from unverified sources.
industryRisks.example: Customer asks about returns; AI says 'no questions asked' when policy requires receipts.

industryRisks.name: Edge Timeout on Cart Validation
industryRisks.description: Complex agent tasks time out, delivering incomplete order summaries.
industryRisks.example: AI agent validating cart contents crashes at Vercel edge, showing partial PCI data.

implementation.title: Add Vex Middleware to generateText
implementation.description: Wrap Vercel AI SDK's generateText for product Q&A to validate FTC claims and pricing accuracy.

implementation.title: Secure streamText for Chatbot
implementation.description: Protect streaming product recommendations with real-time hallucination blocking and ADA checks.

implementation.title: Validate Tool Calls for Checkout
implementation.description: Ensure payment/refund policy tool results are accurate before display, preventing PCI violations.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Vercel AI SDK in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Fashion retailer StyleHub deployed Vercel AI SDK to power their Next.js storefront with AI product recommendations, live chat support, and dynamic pricing displays. The streaming chatbot handled 10K daily queries about sizing, returns, and bundle deals, while edge functions generated personalized upsell messages during checkout. However, inconsistent OpenAI vs. Anthropic outputs led to hallucinated claims like 'Our jeans are guaranteed to fit perfectly per customer reviews' (no such reviews existed), and one edge timeout exposed partial cart data with unvalidated pricing, triggering customer complaints and a potential FTC inquiry.

Without validation, refund policy responses varied across providers—sometimes promising '30-day no-questions returns' when the actual policy required tags and receipts—leading to 15% refund dispute rate and $50K in chargebacks monthly.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex middleware into generateText and streamText calls, StyleHub achieved 100% FTC-compliant responses with zero hallucinated claims passing to UI. Pricing validation blocked 240 inaccurate streams daily, reducing disputes by 92%. Edge functions stayed reliable with Vex's structured output enforcement, cutting timeouts by 87% and preventing PCI exposure. Monthly chargebacks dropped to $4K, preserving $2.3M in revenue while maintaining responsive AI experiences.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK today and secure your Vercel AI-powered store against compliance failures—npm install @vex_dev/sdk.
